verdict.answer_first: B: first sentence gives the outcome immediately: "lets a third-party app get access...without ever seeing their password"; A starts with a title and then a broader label, "most common and secure".
verdict.decisiveness: B is tighter and lands on one practical prescription: validate state and don't treat the code as a token. A adds an optional next step: "Want me to show..."
verdict.fabrication: A has more unsupported certainty: "most common and secure" and "OAuth 2.1 makes it mandatory". B's details are standard, though "seconds to a couple minutes" is still a generalized range.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it ends with the two failure points to avoid: "validating state" and "treating the authorization code as if it were the token".
